---
title: R/xoi User Guide
output: html_document
---

```{r knitr_options, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.width=7, fig.height=4.5)
old_digits <- options("digits")$digits
options(digits=3)
library(broman)
```

<link rel=stylesheet type="text/css" href="https://kbroman.org/stylesheets/kbroman_vignette.css"></script>

<a href="https://github.com/kbroman/xoi"><img src="https://kbroman.org/xoi/xoi_logo.png" align="right" height="138" alt="R/xoi logo"/></a>

[![R-CMD-check](https://github.com/kbroman/xoi/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/kbroman/xoi/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml)
[![CRAN_Status_Badge](https://www.r-pkg.org/badges/version/xoi)](https://cran.r-project.org/package=xoi)
[![r-universe badge](https://kbroman.r-universe.dev/xoi/badges/version)](https://kbroman.r-universe.dev/xoi)
[![zenodo DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/DOI/10.5281/zenodo.3087093.svg)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.3087093)

R/xoi is an R package for the analysis of crossover interference in
experimental crosses, particularly regarding the gamma model.
It has a rather disparate set of functions, which here I seek to
organize and explain.


### Estimates from crossover count data

- [`countxo`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#countxo) -
  - calculate the numbers of crossovers in backcross data

- [`chiasma`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#chiasma) -
  estimate the distribution of the number of chiasmata, given
  crossover count data and the assumption of no chromatid
  interference, and assuming either Poisson counts or unconstrained,
  and with or without an obligate chiasma

### Estimates from crossover location data

- [`find.breaks`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#find.breaks) -
  estimate crossover locations in a backcross

- [`convertxoloc`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#convertxoloc) -
  convert the crossover location data from the format produced by
  `find.breaks()` to that needed for `fitGamma()`

### Estimate the recombination rate along chromosomes

- [`est.recrate`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#est.recrate) -
  obtain a smoothed estimate of the recombination rate along a
  chromosome, from the cM and Mbp positions of markers

- [`recrate2scanone`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#recrate2scanone) -
  convert the output of `est.recrate()` in the the format output by
  `qtl::scanone()`, so it can be plotted with `qtl::plot.scanone()`.


### Measures of interference

- [`est.coi`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#est.coi) -
  estimate the coincidence function from backcross data

- [`coincidence`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#coincidence) -
  an alternative estimate of the coincidence function from backcross data

- [`est.coi.um`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#est.coi.um) -
  estimate the coincidence as a function of micron distance

- [`kfunc`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#kfunc) -
  estimate the one-dimensional version of Ripley's K function


### Data and simulation

- [`bssbsb`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#bssbsb) -
  data from a pair of mouse backcrosses, B6 &times; Spret

- [`simStahl`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#simStahl) -
  simulate data from the Stahl model, an extension to the Gamma model
  where crossovers come from the superposition of two processes, one
  with crossover interference and one without.


### Fit Gamma and Stahl models

- [`fitGamma`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#fitGamma) -
  fit the gamma model for crossover interference from data on
  crossover locations.

- [`fitStahl`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#fitStahl) -
  fit the gamma model for crossover interference from data on
  crossover locations.

- [`stahlLoglik`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#stahlLoglik) -
  calculate the log likelihood for the Stahl model, with data on
  crossover locations.


### Calculations related to the Gamma model

A bunch of calculations related to the Gamma model

- [`xoprob`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#xoprob) -
  probability distribution of the number of crossovers for a
  chromosome of a given length, for the gamma model

- [`ioden`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#ioden) -
  the density of the distance from a given crossover to the next
  crossover, for the gamma model

- [`location.given.one`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#location.given.one) -
  calculate the density function for the location of the crossover
  given that there is precisely one

- [`distance.given.two`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#distance.given.two) -
  the density function for the distance between the two crossovers
  given that there are exactly two

- [`joint.given.two`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#joint.given.two) -
  the joint density function feor the locations of two crossovers
  given that there are exactly two

- [`firstden`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#firstden) -
  the density function of the distance from an arbitrary point to the next
  crossover

- [`first.given.two`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#first.given.two) -
  the density function for the location of the first crossover given
  that there are exactly two

- [`gammacoi`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#gammacoi) -
  the coincidence function for the gamma model

- [`stahlcoi`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#stahlcoi) -
  the coincidence function for the Stahl model

- [`intensity`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/xoi/refman/xoi.html#intensity) -


---

[![CC BY](https://i.creativecommons.org/l/by/3.0/88x31.png)](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0/)
&nbsp; &nbsp; [Karl Broman](https://kbroman.org/)
